author: @JustACogInTheMachine
text:
I'm not sure where to leave this, but I wanted to get this out there. Maybe you can do something with this info.

Roblox recently updated their TOS and privacy policy.

I was about to click agree, as I always do, but hesitated and decided to read through it this once, and I'm glad I did.

By clicking "Agree," I was agreeing to:
Letting roblox steal my robux (my money), gamepasses, items, ect.. without notifying me* 
Acknowledging they aren't going to refund any products unless legally obligated
Waive my right to sue them or join in class action law suits against them

And their Privacy policy now says they can increase the amount of data they take from me without my knowledge.

This is legally binding, even if a minor signs it because by clicking "I agree" it means an adult "acknowledges and accepts all conditions listed" 

*I remember this bit is under section e. Removal of Virtual Content in the TOS.

If you wish to revoke clicking agree, you have to send a PHYSICAL LETTER to roblox within thirty days or else it doesn't count.

I'm sure there's more I didn't see, but I just wanted to let someone know and you might know what to do with this, or who to give it to. 

Thanks
-JustACogInTheMachine

author: @ItzNatzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzz
text:
Story 1.

Hey schlep, not sure if u can catch this predator or this guy is a predator a few years ago, i was playing apex legends with my brother and a guy randomly approached me (btw his user was Mr_Noob) i had vc and he had too so we started chatting. I was 10 by the time, and i wanted friends. He started chatting with me day by day. One day, he asked me for my number and i refused. Then, he gave me his number. We started texting and he always said how he really wanted to make me pregnant and have kids. He sent me a GIF of a woman on top of a man and more innapropriate stuff. He was 25, and saying at the age of 9 i would be a perfect girlfriend, and he even asked me where i live. Of course i didnt say anything. Then he suddenly wanted to facetime... He kept calling me over and over... I responded and he actually looked kinda crappy. He looked like this guy from this video but not exactly like it. He told me he was from Pakistan, and he wanted to meet me irl. I blocked him after that. Still scared of predators...
Story 2.
I was just doing some fxf (fxf means follow x follow basically u follow someone's channel and they follow u back) and this one guy, he was a minor tho, started texting me. It was worse than i thought. I had to get my parents involved. He wouldnt leave me alone, and he would say that he is sorry and he loves me. He sent me n*ked GIFs from where he could find. I still have his number tho, but if hes a minor i cant do anything (He is 14 or 15)
Well, things got worse. He actually asked me for my location, face, and sent me a lot of sus emojis. And he actually told me how he really wants to make kids with me. I was uncomfortable and everytime i told him to f*ck off and leave me alone, he would say "im sorry beautiful, im truly sorry..~"
I never forgave him. He even tried to stalk me.

Im just telling this story for everyone to hear, maybe they have some similar stories!

 From schlep, i just learned a few things i want to tell everyone:
1. Even if somebody called you pretty but u met the person online, dont accept it as a compliment, the person is trying to gather all your trust and trick you.
2. NEVER SHARE YOUR PERSONAL DATA! Even your location can cause danger. Please dont give your location to strangers.
3. If you dont know who to call for help, call your parents. Your parents know better and will help you.
4. Block the person! Yes, i agree, many persons love attention but, its a dangerous thing.
5. NEVER SHOW YOUR FACE!! I did but, hey i was kinda lonely back then and i needed some friends, but please dont do that because its kind of dangerous...

Roblox isnt a safe platform anymore. David promised its a safe game to play, but looks like now, its not anymore..
#FREESCHLEP!!

Stay safe yall

author: @GirlVersusGame
text: 'my life is over' I'm an adult now and I did go through what that guy wanted to do. For years. It's not some innocent little once of with those people and it doesn't just affect you there and then. You can feel nothing, then years later when you do have a real partner and your life is on track something will click and everything you experienced will come rushing back. It will steal the very breath from your lungs. You'll question everything from who you are, what you are into and where those preferences even came from. When an adult interacts with a child on that level they rewrite your understanding of what a relationship is. As an adult I'd call that brainwashing. If that's not bad enough I did return to online gaming (years later) and who did I run into but another person like that only this person was selling his children for in-game credit. He received six years and served three. I felt like a f---- idiot for making friends with him and for chatting with his daughters on mic and never picking up on what they were going through. When he did get out he immediately contacted me with a sob story about how the defense in America is underfunded. He took no responsibility for his actions. I love gaming (as my username probably suggests) but I find it impossible to play anything online now. Or to trust people. That's the kind of legacy those people leave behind, I think they just don't care.

author: @GillgameshConsumerOfReality
text:
Imma tell a personal story and it actually gives me faith in humanity.

So I was playing Minecraft Hypixel. Just having a good time and I met someone there who I’ll call Terry. Terry was cool, he was funny and smart and we quickly became friends, we got each other’s Skype and started talking and it was all pretty normal, just two people having fun online. Then I said that I needed to go to school the next day and he said “wait, how old are you.” Turns out we thought we were similar ages to each other since our humour was so similar but it turns out I befriended a 25 year old when I was 13, it was a complete accident on both of our parts and as soon as our ages became known to us we immediately stopped talking and removed each other. I remember us leaving on pretty good terms, being kind of bummed that our age gap was Waaay too big for us to be friends. Yeah, I haven’t seen or heard from him since and when my brain developed more I gained a lot of respect for Terry since he instantly stopped everything to do with me when he found out my age at the time. Terry was cool, I kinda miss him sometimes.

Hi schlep! I am itsuki, I am obsessed with what you are doing, I enjoy watching you get people arrested ‘cause of what they do. You actually help! You are one of my fav YouTubers too! I’m already subcribed <3 

Schlep is the best because it captures a uniquely human blend of effort, endurance, and humor that few other words manage to communicate. It doesn’t pretend that work is always elegant or efficient; instead, it embraces the reality that sometimes you have to drag yourself, your bags, and your responsibilities from point A to point B with nothing but determination and maybe a sigh. There’s charm in the honesty of it—a wink at the universal struggle of getting through the day. To ‘schlep’ is to acknowledge that life isn’t always streamlined, but you’re moving forward anyway. It’s a word with texture, personality, and an almost affectionate self-awareness. In a world that often glorifies hustle and polish, schlep reminds us that perseverance can be messy, unglamorous, and still completely admirable.

author: @RRxf-l5b
text:
I’ve been playing Roblox since I was 10 years old. Now I’m 20. Roblox shaped a huge part of my life, my imagination, and my memories. But today, I am filled with sadness and rage. Sexual predators have poisoned this platform. They ruin the game, destroy trust, and hurt innocent people. They are parasites, monsters who have no place here or anywhere else. The only place for sexual predators is in prison.
Because of them, adult players are often seen with suspicion, as if loving Roblox at 20 years old makes us criminals. But we are NOT them. We are a generation that grew up with Roblox, who still love it for what it represents: creativity, sharing, and fun.
Predators deserve nothing but to be permanently banned, erased from this community they destroy. Roblox deserves better. The community deserves better. And the adult players who still love this game deserve respect — not to be lumped in with these scum.

Im happy that you help cleaning of those scum thank you for all you did schlep

author: @TheRobloxActivist
text:
If you’ve ever wondered how Roblox even became a billion dollar company, just ask the millions of innocent people they took money from after deleting their accounts for no valid reason. You should see this one guy’s followers list; I witnessed as they kept banning many innocent people. Half of the players I know on Roblox are now deleted. I saw a whole list of 500 people wiped out.

A 10 year old Roblox account got deleted in 2021 for being “toxic,” and Roblox didn’t even give the owner a warning. That’s really a wild ban, because most of you who play Roblox know that everyone on Roblox is toxic to some degree. A great amount of people who play Roblox are guaranteed to be toxic. Roblox hides the fact that they do not care about the issues that really matter, they do not tolerate any form of bullying or toxic behavior now. This all started because of one incident where a kid was bullied online and the parents sued roblox for it.

Roblox is fed up with everything. They now have the power to do anything they want on this platform. They can drop the most terrible update imaginable, and it still wouldn’t stop people from playing. I’m sure there are a lot of people who aren’t happy about what’s really happening on Roblox right now. The platform has seriously gone downhill, and no one is doing anything to fix the problem.

We already know the CEO of Roblox needs to retire. At this point he is just another problem and he is too old to be running a gaming platform and only making things worse. The image he created years ago has faded, and now that he’s older, his main goal is just to profit as much as possible off the platform. He doesn’t really care about what players want, because for him it all comes down to money. The people on the platform are no better, with my 13 years of experience, I have watched as most of the people on Roblox also only care about profit and they won't stop at nothing to gain profit on Roblox. I have footage of being scammed by a Roblox developer, I have many other footage that I collected over the years, with my so called social experiments, where I would test the players ability to not scam. I have been scammed way too many times to count, and I filmed most of the people who scammed me. It's crazy to think that many other scammers on Roblox are getting out of control. Roblox refuses to give an account back to the rightful owner of the account after It's been sold. That new policy now restricts people who created the accounts they made originally and makes it so they cannot ever get the account back. The CEO of Roblox, on the other hand, is too old to be running a gaming platform, and he’s turned it into something else entirely.

author: @EssaBuds
text:
Hey schlep

just wanted to say thank you for the work you’re doing exposing predators on Roblox and other online spaces. What you’re doing is so important — and it really means a lot to people like me.
When I was just 8 years old, I was almost lured out of my home by someone I met on Roblox. They acted like a friend at first, but eventually started asking me to meet them in real life. Thankfully, something didn’t feel right, and I told my mom before anything worse could happen. But it was a terrifying experience that I didn’t fully understand at the time — and I now realize how dangerous it could have been.
Now that I've been Watching your videos years later it helped me realize I wasn’t alone. You're not just catching predators — you're protecting kids and raising awareness in a way that really matters. I hope more people see your content and learn how serious and real these threats can be. I've taken my little sister off of roblox to keep her safe thank you schlep. I could go on and on and on about how thankful I am for you. Thank you for being a hero

author: @puck4063
text:
Other online games for children deal with the same issue. I was groomed on Moviestarplanet or Gosupermodel aswell. The internet is a dangerous place for vulnerable children. Predators know how to manipulate well and they will tell you what you want to hear. I didnt get a lot of affection at home and the predators I talked with always made me feel loved and special - I craved the attention and affection, not knowing that I was being manipulated for their sick desires. 
I really dont know what the solution would be though. Probably parents talking to children about online dangers regularly and trying to stay connected with them... but like i said: predators usually talk to the vulnerable ones, that aren't being protected by their parents and have some problems (psychological or family issues). 
It's so sad and I hope that everyone who ever experienced grooming can recover ❤

text: The bolded lines contain several instances of figurative language. First, there is an example of personification where "the night would be my eyelids," giving the night a human characteristic. Secondly, the phrase "it was like trying to see through a blanket" is a simile, comparing the difficulty of seeing to the experience of looking through a thick blanket using the word "like". Finally, "lights of the yacht became faint and ever-vanishing fireflies" is a metaphor, directly comparing the yacht's lights to fireflies without using "like" or "as." author: @pyrrhic8976
text:
2:31 is annoying as hell, it's a nitpick but all of the names flashing by being girls and the need to passively ignore male victims, or female victims of female preds, (don't even get me started on male victims of female preds, which yall will never acknowledge even by accident) is such a huge part of rape culture. 

This whole culture of "vigilantes" who represent only 1 or 2 group of victims is starting to get annoying, all of these apps and even real life places teem with female predators, but even putting aside the capturing of them (which is harder because of entrapment laws), acting oblivious to their existence is infuriating, I seriously wonder if yall actually care about the issue and aren't just accidentally helping as a side effect of content creation as opposed to the other way around. It seriously seems like people just pick this niche up for views and go "well if we help with the issue that's cool too!"

If your convictions stop at surface level awareness spreading then they aren't actually convictions, it's like when Mr Beast plays the passive centrist role on the most clear cut of divisive issues to not accidentally lose 1% of his fans, except y'all have NOTHING to lose by being inclusive, I wish this community would do better.

author: @pastelnut027
text:
When I was 14, I met this 19-20 guy on Google+ and we briefly interacted. It was when I turned 15 a few months later that he reached out more and we actually started talking and became a couple. (Thinking about it now, he might’ve been waiting for Romeo and Juliet laws to apply or whatever.)
He sent explicit pictures and we talked almost daily. Hell, I remember one time he asked if we could do stuff over the phone. Thank god I came up with an excuse to not because I felt uncomfortable. I remember trying to keep things legal until I turned 18 but he never tried to do the same. It was several months later that I woke up from a nightmare about our “relationship” and the explicit photos he sent that I was snapped into reality about how messed up it was. I immediately went to my mother to tell her about what was going on and after a short scolding we later called the police to tell them about what was going on. I gave them his phone number, but the only thing that happened was the operator telling us to “just block him”. It’s not like he lived in a different state, he lived a few hours away, I feel like they could’ve passed that on to the city he lived in.

I was a wreck afterwards and was terrified that he’d come down to my town to come find me because he knew what town I lived in (and vice versa). I developed ptsd from it, and while I’m mostly stable now at 23, I still have episodes sometimes. I wish something was actually done, and I’ve only recently heard about you thanks to the current drama, I’m at least glad something was done for all the potential victims of these creeps.

author: @HotSeat17
text:
Hosea 4:6-7
[6]My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge. Because you have rejected knowledge, I also will reject you from being priest for Me; Because you have forgotten the law of your God, I also will forget your children.
[7]“The more they increased, The more they sinned against Me; I will change their glory into shame."
Jesus warns against causing a "little one" who believes in Him to stumble, stating, "it would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck, and he were thrown into the sea" (Matthew 18:6, Mark 9:42, Luke 17:2). In the Old Testament, Deuteronomy 24:6 prohibits taking a debtor's millstone, a tool essential for daily sustenance, as collateral, reflecting God's concern for the vulnerable.
Millstones were vital for preparing food in ancient agrarian societies, and this prohibition protected a person's basic means of survival. It demonstrates God's deep concern for justice, compassion, and ensuring that economic dealings do not exploit the poor or vulnerable.
Weight and Judgment:
In the New Testament, the millstone symbolizes the heavy burden and severe consequences of sin and causing others to stumble, leading to inevitable divine judgment. 
Vulnerability and Protection:
The Deuteronomy passage highlights the millstone as a symbol of vulnerability, underscoring the need to protect those who are struggling.

author: @Highellothere
text: This shows a LOT about what’s wrong with society. Ofc one huge problem is that huge historic businesses in GENERAL, with Roblox and Apple being examples, have managers that are too money hungry to care and do anything about these issues - but what gets too overlooked is the issue CAUSING *this* issue. There’s a loneliness epidemic, stemming from covid’s lockdown, and also the overarching problem: the profiteering of the internet. Ofc, the root issue of this is human greed, which we (humans) have tried to sort out before using ideologies such as communism, but the only way i can think of right now to solve all these root issues is positive indoctrination of society from a young age. So, if u read to the end of this comment, spread the word and form ur own perspective (based on facts Ofc).

author: @critter_core
text:
It's absolutely horrible...

But I have to admit that watching these videos, I'm REALLY glad that Schlep and JiDion have that sort of 'interview' moment with the predators, cause man do you learn a lot. You really get to see what's going on sometimes, and I've always found the criminal-to-reason thing very interesting (as disturbing as it is...)

Like, fessing up that he started doing this after the break up, you can hear how absolutely insecure he is... And like, of course that's not an excuse, but you'll hear this fact with so many other predators, or other criminals, and especially with killers, how there's often some kind of reason (it's often severe trauma, especially from their parents and ESPECIALLY with serial killers).

There's like,,, almost always this 'something' that makes them go down that dark path. 

Like I said, certainly not an excuse, but very interesting. Once you hear it, you just keep seeing it. Though I will say, it seems like a lot of predators DO use their insecurities and anxieties as an excuse and definitely seem a little too willing to do so.

I didn't really watch Schlep's videos until the roblox situation happened, and truly, he and JiDion are doing amazing work. It's horrible that Roblox would rather try (and miserably fail) to protect its image over making the platform safer. Roblox could have actually started looking great if they did take those reports seriously, if they HELPED these people take down these predators. 

But they just had to take the wrong path, now didn't they :')

author: @LowkIdSmashElliotFromForsaken
text:
High school Freshman here. 

This is just.. I can't believe it. This is such a low. This person is so pathetic...

The fact he continued on with this knowing Sophie was 13 is insane. It's always the same excuse. Feeling empty, lonely, loss of a partner. That's not an excuse to switch your sights to children. THIRTEEN. He is 21 in this video, five years shy of twice Sophie's age. 

Not only did he continue knowing she was 13, but knowing what he was doing was wrong, morally, and legally. Even asking if Sophie was an undercover cop. He knew what he was doing could have dire consequences if caught and still continued. These people are sickening. Knowing this is a horrible thing in every possible way and yet continuing, as long as you feel you aren't of risk of being caught. He didn't really care if what he was doing was wrong. He only cared if he'd feel the repercussions.

Not only did he send photos of his 'burrito', he even tried to expose this 13 year old girl to marijuana too 'soothe her down'.

People like this sicken me. They're just like predators. They wait for prey, anything they can take advantage of, and then they strike. 

We all need to sit for a moment or two too appreciate Schlep. I've not watched his videos, but I can say that every time a predator falls for a decoy, that's a single child saved. Schlep is saving children on this platform that I'm sure some of you use daily, or at least very often. Every time Schlep catches someone, that not only is one, but potentially many 13 - 17 year old girls just like Sophie, that don't partake in illegal sexual activities with an adult.

Schlep, I appreciate you for all the work you've done to protect the children on ROBLOX from predators that take advantage of children. Even if you've discontinued this work due to 'issues', we appreciate all you've done for us, and all the children you've saved. 

Remember, any child watching this, or even an adult, every time a predator isn't caught, you or someone you know and love could fall victim to a predator. It always happens to 'someone else'. And we're all 'someone else' to somebody...

author: @cupcakecatcaterson8884
text: When I was like 11 or 12 my brother's older friends (although under 18) sexually harrassed me. They sent me explicit texts (thankfully no photos) and I was harrassed. They had a group chat where they would often harass me. My brother was in it. They never said anything sexually explicit to me in the gc but outside was a different story. I tried to tell the other people in the group chat what was happening and they didn't believe me and called it "not harrassment". I knew what that stuff was at the time (for some part of the whole) but it traumatizes me to this day. I'm asexual now. I didn't tell my parents because I was ashamed. I would try to leave and they would keep adding me back in. One day, they finally let me go. And that was the best day of my life. I cried. These people took me to the brink of soupicide. The only reason I didn't do it was because I was too scared to and couldn't figure out how to do it in a painless way. I'm better now. Well, as better as you can be. (Disclaimer: I do not blame my brother. He didn't know, he is mid functioning autistic so he just wanted to fit in.)

men on roblox make me and my friends SO uncomfortable and we’re 22 and only really play blade ball and horror games. i can’t imagine the things that happen and are said to actual innocent kids who don’t know better. when i showed my friend this channel, she kept mentioning the game ‘dollhouse’ that a lot of i guess age players and kids go in to role play. 

we were in there maybe 5 mins just to see the atmosphere since we had never been in there, and within seconds of joining we had dudes swarming us, asking us if we’re minors/what are ages where etc. we let them believe we were children just checking out diff games and it only got weirder from there. 

it’s scary seeing this as a children’s game and yet all these things are openly going on and roblox rarely ever does anything for it. i hope my niece stays her ass on grow a garden.

author: @dumpaccount001
text:
When I was 13, I was new to Discord and had no idea how it worked. I didn’t even know how I got into certain servers, they just kind of appeared, and I was clueless about how anything ran there. One of them ended up being some kind of "for under 18 dating server". I didn’t click on anything weird, but I do remember choosing roles, and one of them was “open DMs,” which basically let random people message me.

One day, a guy DMed me saying he was 18 and asked how old I was. He sent me a picture — ginger hair, wearing sunglasses. I sent him one too, but it only showed half of my face. Then he said, “You’re too f*king hot for 13,” and that he was going to do stuff with my photo. I told him, “Idk why, but I’m starting to feel uncomfortable now, sorry.” He replied saying “My phone is sticky” and “You did this to me.” I messaged my friend right away, and they told me to block him. Before I did, he kept saying more gross stuff like “It’s still coming out tf.” I finally blocked him, but I accidentally reopened the chat, and he saw the typing bubble. Then he goes, “Are you ok with me slitting my wrist?”

Thankfully, I listened to my friend. They told me to copy and paste this to shut him up “Dude stop, I’m not tryna get with yo ugly chubby ginger lookin ass.” 

I also reported him to the server admin, and he got banned. I trusted my gut, and I’m lucky I got away from that. I really hope he hasn’t done that to anyone else since.

author: @Minorsquadtrio
text:
Hello, this account is shared by the squad but I’d like to go anonymously.

This is a true story that happened to me
———————————————————————
So this was a long time ago, I was 8 and I really loved Roblox. I also loved to try out new games and make friends. At the moment I was looking for something to play bc I was getting bored and wanted to find something fun to do! I searched up hangout games bc I wanted to make friends!  Anyways while I was in the game and I’m pretty sure it was a club game, I think you can see where I’m going with this.. a guy came up to me and said that I looked cool and wanted to be my friend. I said yes ofc and we were talk for a few weeks, he seemed so sweet and nice but little did I know what was coming next.. it was a normal day after school, I finished my homework so I went online to play with him. While we were talking though out this new week he was pretty weird and acted different from usual.. I was talking to him and then he asked me REALLY weird questions about my life, luckily I told my mom bc I had a weird feeling and when she read the messages she was disgusted by his acts. She blocked and unfriended him from my account and now I know more about internet safety. I will never get discord bc you never know what can happen on that platform.. ever since I’ve been trying to protect my younger friends from people like them, they feel like I’m a mom to them so I feel that if they’re parents aren’t gonna do anything I’ll step in and be the parent that WILL protect them from online harassment/predators..
—————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
Everything I just said was a true story and I said this bc I want YOU ALL to know about this bc this is just another example of a predator that will use trust to get you to believe that their a nice person

author: @KittycatmeowBurger
text: Um. So this incident recently just happened. I met 2 people in a Roblox game and 1 was a boy and the other was a girl. We talked for a bit and they said their ages, 14 turning 15. The girl told the boy that they would look good together, he responded with lol. Which I kind of questioned, but I didn’t say anything. So we all added each other and made a group chat and that was it. Two days later, she told us that she had something to tell us and she said she was 22 years old. So me wanting to be a criminal prosecutor when I grow up, I went off on her. Then the boy private messaged me and told me that he couldn’t believe it. He didn’t know what to do and also that she told him other stuff as well. That same day we all met, but I had left early. So he said when I was gone, she said stuff like, he was cute, he made her blush, and that they would look good together. So I went back to the group chat and confronted her saying let’s hop on vc. She questioned it, but I told her wanting a clear explanation and answer isn’t weird. But she refused. Then she told us it was all a joke saying she saw it on social media, which I find funny because I never seen any video with someone pretending to be an older person talking to a minor as a joke. So on my account Scout4L1F3 which is banned for now because of him, I decided to play a little bit of "Warm Isolation" it was fun and stuff, i was using my soundboard js for a lil bit of trolling and that's when Steve_Bloks came in, his display was "Lua" for some reason, idk, but his avatar was very feminine, IDK, but anyways, he said i was the only normal one, yk for chats, hanging out, ETC, we played for a while having fun and stuff until when he turned on his mic, and it was bad, now I'm not trying to be racist or anything, but he sounded Indian and in his 20's now this made me feel like he was one of "those" pedos, because in the game, there are custom books you can get to have a title on it, the title for his book was "r34" or "femboys" this made me feel icky a lot, so I ""DISCONNECTED"" and joined  dif server, and he started chatting me telling me to join back all in some feminy way, anyways, I rejoin, and I heard his full voice, and it was bad, after that, I blocked him, unfriended him, and never tried to go back to his account ever again. Luckily like I did say though he did get banned or js deleted his account but let me tell you something that you should look out for. Make sure when you talk to someone, examine the avatar and the actual name for the account, not the display, but actual, this is so that you know who you are talking to and to make sure you don't get fooled, sometimes it wont really work, but its the best option, make sure that it looks like they aren't trying to sweet talk you like by acting friendly or anything, this could often lead to you joining them in priv servers, and lastly, badges or creations, looking at the badges they have can tell you about the personality they have, it makes it so you know damn well who you are actually talking to, that's all I really know, hope this story can help you or anything like that. Stay safe out their Robloxians. -VintageDustSpeacks

text: Im glad theres people like you guys here. When i was 11, i made a friend online who was about 15 or 16. They were normal at first, but we stopped talking and almost year went by. We started talking again, and i was still 11. They were completely different people. You could probably guess that being a little kid, i never told anyone. So the guy is still roaming, but im pretty sure he's changed, i talked to him when i was 21 backwards but he kept apologizing. Out of fear, i blocked him. But it still doesn't get the images out of my brain. (Im 31 backward now if you're wondering) i would sometimes think that it didn't affect me, but it obviously did. Since i was and still am a minor, my brain is still developing, and i shouldn't have seen that. When i was 11, i would fear every day at the end of school, walking out of school in fear. knowing that he's still going to be there asking for pictures. I never did luckily. And since my brain was and still is developing, i used to be annoying and loud but since then when it happened, i stopped being inmature, at school everyone would be loud and immature at everything, thinking everything is funny. author: @Sans.cant.keep.dodging.forever
text:
For those who don't know

The F-16 Fighting Falcon is a compact, multi-role fighter aircraft. It is highly maneuverable and has proven itself in air-to-air combat and air-to-surface attack. It provides a relatively low-cost, high-performance weapon system for the United States and allied nations.

In an air combat role, the F-16's maneuverability and combat radius (distance it can fly to enter air combat, stay, fight and return) exceed that of all potential threat fighter aircraft. It can locate targets in all weather conditions and detect low flying aircraft in radar ground clutter. In an air-to-surface role, the F-16 can fly more than 500 miles (860 kilometers), deliver its weapons with superior accuracy, defend itself against enemy aircraft, and return to its starting point. An all-weather capability allows it to accurately deliver ordnance during non-visual bombing conditions.

In designing the F-16, advanced aerospace science and proven reliable systems from other aircraft such as the F-15 and F-111 were selected. These were combined to simplify the airplane and reduce its size, purchase price, maintenance costs and weight. The light weight of the fuselage is achieved without reducing its strength. With a full load of internal fuel, the F-16 can withstand up to nine G's -- nine times the force of gravity -- which exceeds the capability of other current fighter aircraft.

The cockpit and its bubble canopy give the pilot unobstructed forward and upward vision, and greatly improved vision over the side and to the rear. The seat-back angle was expanded from the usual 13 degrees to 30 degrees, increasing pilot comfort and gravity force tolerance. The pilot has excellent flight control of the F-16 through its "fly-by-wire" system. Electrical wires relay commands, replacing the usual cables and linkage controls. For easy and accurate control of the aircraft during high G-force combat maneuvers, a side stick controller is used instead of the conventional center-mounted stick. Hand pressure on the side stick controller sends electrical signals to actuators of flight control surfaces such as ailerons and rudder.

Avionics systems include a highly accurate enhanced global positioning and inertial navigation systems, or EGI, in which computers provide steering information to the pilot. The plane has UHF and VHF radios plus an instrument landing system. It also has a warning system and modular countermeasure pods to be used against airborne or surface electronic threats. The fuselage has space for additional avionics systems.

The F-16A, a single-seat model, first flew in December 1976. The first operational F-16A was delivered in January 1979 to the 388th Tactical Fighter Wing at Hill Air Force Base, Utah.

The F-16B, a two-seat model, has tandem cockpits that are about the same size as the one in the A model. Its bubble canopy extends to cover the second cockpit. To make room for the second cockpit, the forward fuselage fuel tank and avionics growth space were reduced. During training, the forward cockpit is used by a student pilot with an instructor pilot in the rear cockpit.

All F-16s delivered since November 1981 have built-in structural and wiring provisions and systems architecture that permit expansion of the multirole flexibility to perform precision strike, night attack and beyond-visual-range interception missions. This improvement program led to the F-16C and F-16D aircraft, which are the single- and two-place counterparts to the F-16A/B, and incorporate the latest cockpit control and display technology. All active units and many Air National Guard and Air Force Reserve units have converted to the F-16C/D.

The F-16 was built under an unusual agreement creating a consortium between the United States and four NATO countries: Belgium, Denmark, the Netherlands and Norway. These countries jointly produced with the United States an initial 348 F-16s for their air forces. Final airframe assembly lines were located in Belgium and the Netherlands. The consortium's F-16s are assembled from components manufactured in all five countries. Belgium also provides final assembly of the F100 engine used in the European F-16s. Recently, Portugal joined the consortium. The long-term benefits of this program will be technology transfer among the nations producing the F-16, and a common-use aircraft for NATO nations. This program increases the supply and availability of repair parts in Europe and improves the F-16's combat readiness.

U.S. Air Force F-16 multirole fighters were deployed to the Persian Gulf in 1991 in support of Operation Desert Storm, where more sorties were flown than with any other aircraft. These fighters were used to attack airfields, military production facilities, Scud missiles sites and a variety of other targets.

During Operation Allied Force, U.S. Air Force F-16 multirole fighters flew a variety of missions to include suppression of enemy air defense, offensive counter air, defensive counter air, close air support and forward air controller missions. Mission results were outstanding as these fighters destroyed radar sites, vehicles, tanks, MiGs and buildings.

Since Sept. 11, 2001, the F-16 has been a major component of the combat forces committed to the war on terrorism flying thousands of sorties in support of operations Noble Eagle (Homeland Defense), Enduring Freedom in Afghanistan and Iraqi Freedom

General characteristics
Primary function: multirole fighter
Contractor: Lockheed Martin Corp.
Power plant: F-16C/D: one Pratt and Whitney F100-PW-200/220/229 or General Electric F110-GE-100/129
Thrust: F-16C/D, 27,000 pounds
Wingspan: 32 feet, 8 inches (9.8 meters)
Length: 49 feet, 5 inches (14.8 meters)
Height: 16 feet (4.8 meters)
Weight: 19,700 pounds without fuel (8,936 kilograms)  
Maximum takeoff weight: 37,500 pounds (16,875 kilograms)  
Fuel capacity: 7,000 pounds internal (3,175 kilograms); typical capacity, 12,000 pounds with two external tanks (5443 kilograms)
Payload: two 2,000-pound bombs, two AIM-9, two AIM-120 and two 2400-pound external fuel tanks
Speed: 1,500 mph (Mach 2 at altitude)Range: more than 2,002 miles ferry range (1,740 nautical miles)
Ceiling: above 50,000 feet (15 kilometers)
Armament: one M-61A1 20mm multibarrel cannon with 500 rounds; external stations can carry up to six air-to-air missiles, conventional air-to-air and air-to-surface munitions and electronic countermeasure pods
Crew: F-16C, one; F-16D, one or two
Unit cost: F-16A/B , $14.6 million (fiscal 98 constant dollars); F-16C/D,$18.8 million (fiscal 98 constant dollars)

author: @AugustAtrocities
text:
Schools don't want anyone to know about this, but one of the main things that turns people into school shooters and child predators is how a lot of schools are completely negligent towards the mental health of their students(which is literally 99.9% minors). There kids out there who seriously struggle with socializing with other kids, and school districts never try to always let every single student know that they need to make friends and be inclusive to other people, no matter how sketchy they may be. That's basically what happened with me; my classmates, teachers and even my parents were during my around 15 years of being in school, completely negligent towards me and especially my mental health. This is why my community has been turned against me.

Believe it or not, people with bad mental health are more likely to do really bad things. This doesn't excuse any criminals that did their deplorable acts because of how they were treated in school. This also doesn't mean that victims of their school(s) negligence can use that to justify every bad thing they do. If schools start encouraging their students to make friends and be inclusive as often as they bring up math, it probably isn't gonna fix crime but should absolutely go to great lengths to suppress the amount of school shooters and child predators kids may have to deal with. The more these systems completely discourage people from becoming child predators, the safer kids will feel, the less likely they are to have their childhoods ruined, the better everyone on the internet in the future can feel.

And don't you fucking dare say I'm "lying", "yapping", or "going on a schizophrenic ramble". I am confident that this will do a great job at keeping future childhoods safer from various cases of child predation.
